workflow_name: Clang-tidy static analysis (Ubuntu 24.04, Python 3.12, Clang-18, Clang-tidy-18)
author: bumbosiepsak
title: RISCV64 Docker build fails - libpython3-dev:riscv64 has unresolvable dependencies on libtinfo6:riscv64
signature: apt-get install libpython3-dev:riscv64 fails - libpython3.10-stdlib:riscv64 depends on libtinfo6:riscv64 but not installable|Configuration
key_errors: E: Unable to correct problems, you have held broken packages.
libpython3.10-stdlib:riscv64 : Depends: libncursesw6:riscv64 (>= 6.1) but it is not going to be installed
libpython3.10-stdlib:riscv64 : Depends: libtinfo6:riscv64 (>= 6) but it is not going to be installed
libreadline8:riscv64 : Depends: libtinfo6:riscv64 (>= 6) but it is not going to be installed

ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/sh -c dpkg --add-architecture riscv64 && apt-get update -o Dir::Etc::sourcelist=/etc/apt/sources.list.d/riscv64-sources.list && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends libpython3-dev:riscv64" did not complete successfully: exit code: 100
root_cause: The ubuntu_22_04_riscv Docker image build fails at Dockerfile line 72 (step 5/8) when attempting to install libpython3-dev:riscv64 via cross-compilation. APT cannot resolve the dependency chain: libpython3.10-stdlib:riscv64 requires libncursesw6:riscv64 and libtinfo6:riscv64 which are not going to be installed. The likely cause is that the amd64 Python packages installed from the deadsnakes PPA (libpython3.10-dev 3.10.12) hold a newer version constraint that conflicts with the riscv64 counterparts available in ubuntu-ports jammy. This is the same failure seen in prior run 29022024794 for the same PR 36774, also in the merge queue.
